prompt: A SmartPark Media Operations management system with main features including offline-first parking device event ingestion, a consumer-grade media library for drivers and onsite staff, playlist creation and sharing, explainable recommendations, asset governance, account controls, and operational monitoring. The primary roles are Regular User (onsite staff/front-desk operator), Administrator (operations/content manager), and Field Technician (device diagnostics and ingestion validation). The Vue.js interface uses frontend routing to separate Search, Library, Favorites, Playlists, Now Playing, Admin Console, and Device Console views, with responsive layouts optimized for kiosk tablets and front-desk desktops over the local network. Regular Users sign in with a locally verified username and password, browse a library of audio announcements and short clips, save favorites, build and edit playlists, and share playlists internally using a copyable on-screen code that can be entered on another device without external messaging. The search experience supports full-text queries, filters for tags, duration (for example, under 2 minutes), and recency (last 30 days), plus sorting by Most Played, Newest, and Recommended. A Now Playing panel shows recent plays and session history, and recommendations include simple on-screen explanations such as Based on your favorites: Safety, Overnight, Gate Issues. Administrators use an admin console to review and approve uploads, manage blacklisted accounts, freeze accounts for a defined period such as 72 hours, prevent deletion of assets that are referenced by playlists until replaced, and view dashboards for device status, queue backlog, API health, and content usage. Field Technicians use a device console to verify that gate, camera, and geomagnetic sensor events are being received, with explicit statuses for duplicates, late arrivals, replays, and buffered transmissions when local node connectivity is intermittent. The backend is implemented with Laravel exposing REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js frontend over the local network, with MySQL as the system of record for users, roles, playlists, favorites, play history, search index tables, media asset metadata, and parking device events. Parking device integration is provided through reserved local interfaces including HTTP ingestion endpoints and optional file-drop folders for cameras, gates, and sensors; each event includes a device identifier, event type, event timestamp, monotonic sequence counter, and unique idempotency key. The server deduplicates events within a 7-day window, corrects out-of-order sequences using per-device monotonic counters, accepts controlled replay with audit trails, and avoids double-applying side effects during retransmission. If the device gateway is offline, it buffers up to 10,000 events locally and retransmits using exponential backoff. Media assets are stored on local disk with reference tracking, local thumbnail generation, and multi-size crops such as 160, 480, and 960 pixel widths. Uploads enforce allowlists for JPEG, PNG, PDF, MP3, and MP4, with size caps of 25 MB for images/documents and 250 MB for video, plus MIME sniffing and file fingerprint validation to block disguised executables; reserved hooks exist for future on-prem scanning tools without requiring internet connectivity. Asynchronous jobs handled by Laravel queues perform thumbnail generation, indexing, recommendation candidate generation, metrics rollups, and purge jobs. The system automatically degrades by disabling Recommended ranking and falling back to Most Played when API p95 latency exceeds 800 ms for 5 minutes or recommendation hit rate drops below 10 percent. Security includes salted password hashing, at-rest encryption for sensitive fields such as email when stored for profile purposes, masked logs, soft-delete with a 30-day purge job for deleted accounts, and a local monitoring page with no third-party telemetry that surfaces API error rate, queue backlog, storage health, and device ingestion health.
database: MySQL (primary), Redis (cache/queues), Local disk storage for media assets
